Input: 1. Open TruBlue app from fresh install 2. Verify all UI elements are present
Expected Output: Screen displays verification prompt with checkmark icon

Test Case Description: Get Verified Popup - Don't Miss Out Modal
Input: 1. Tap Verify Selfie button 2. Verify modal content 3. Test X button, Skip and Verify Selfie buttons
Expected Output: Modal popup appears with warning icon displaying all benefits and restrictions clearly

Input: 1. Proceed to selfie capture 2. Grant camera permission if requested 3. Verify UI elements
Expected Output: Camera view initializes with circular frame
column7: instructions visible. Live video feed displays in capture frame

Input: 1. Position face clearly in circular frame 2. Ensure proper lighting (well-lit, no shadows) 3. Remove sunglasses/masks 4. Keep camera steady 5. Tap capture button
Expected Output: Image captures successfully and processes. Wait Processing screen displays. Verification completes with success screen showing benefits and TrueCheck badge awarded

Input: 1. Position face partially out of frame 2. Cover face with hands 3. Turn away from camera 4. Attempt to capture
Expected Output: System prevents invalid capture or flags image. Error message displays: Your face is not visible. UI shows Poor Visibility vs Good Visibility examples. Try Again button available for retry

Input: 1. Capture with sunglasses on (blocking eyes) 2. Capture with mask covering lower face 3. Capture in poor lighting
Expected Output: Image submitted but quality check fails. Error message displays: Poor quality or similar. Instructions provided for improvement. Update Selfie button available

Input: 1. After valid capture submit image 2. Observe processing screen 3. Wait for completion
Expected Output: Wait Processing screen appears with loading indicator. Message displays: Give us a bit to do our magic and we'll get you on your way. Transitions to success or failure screen

Input: 1. Submit valid clear selfie 2. Wait for verification to complete 3. Observe results screen
Expected Output: Success screen displays with checkmark. Congratulations title appears. All 4 benefits listed with checkmarks. TrueCheck gold badge visible on profile. Continue button navigates to feed

Input: 1. Submit selfie that fails verification 2. Observe error screen 3. Review retry options
Expected Output: Failure screen displays with X icon. Message: Selfie verification failed. Retry requirements shown. Buttons available: Old Selfie

Input: 1. Access document verification 2. Verify selection options 3. Test each button functionality
Expected Output: Choose an option popup appears. Options displayed: Take a Photo
column6: Upload from Gallery buttons. Cancel button closes popup. Each option properly routes to next step

Input: 1. Select Take a Photo 2. Position document in frame 3. Align with capture frame 4. Tap capture button
Expected Output: Camera opens with document frame guide. Instruction text: Take a photo of your document's photo page. Document captures and validates. Proceeds to back side capture

Input: 1. After front capture flip document 2. Position back side in frame 3. Align correctly 4. Tap capture button
Expected Output: Instruction appears: Take a photo of the back side of your document. Back side captures and validates. System confirms both sides ready for verification

Input: 1. Submit passport images (both sides) 2. Ensure clear photo and ID number visibility 3. Verify document not expired
Expected Output: System identifies as valid passport. Document content verified. Expiration date checked. Verification succeeds. Success screen displays with congratulations message and benefits

Test Case Description: Document Type Validation - Invalid Document Type
Input: 1. Submit non-accepted document 2. Observe system response 3. Review error handling
Expected Output: System analyzes and detects unsupported type. Error message: Please use another Identity document. Popup lists accepted types (Passport
column7: Driver's License). Try Again button available

Input: 1. Capture blurry document image 2. Submit for verification 3. Observe quality check
Expected Output: System analyzes image quality. Quality check fails. Error message: Blurry Image - Ensure details are sharp and legible. Reference Guide available. Try Again button returns to capture

Input: 1. Capture with harsh glare/reflection 2. Submit image 3. Observe detection
Expected Output: System detects lighting issues. Error message: Glare & Lighting - Avoid bright lights reflecting on document. Tips for better lighting provided. Try Again button available

Input: 1. Submit valid but unverifiable document 2. Wait for verification 3. Observe error handling
Expected Output: Processing completes unsuccessfully. Error screen: We couldn't identify your document. Suggestions provided: Blurry Image and Glare & Lighting tips. Try Again button for retry

Input: 1. Submit valid, clear, properly-positioned document 2. Wait for system verification 3. Observe success screen
Expected Output: Verification succeeds. Success screen displays with checkmark. Congratulations! Your ID is verified. All benefits displayed with checkmarks. ID-verified badge awarded. Continue button works

Input: 1. Complete both selfie and document verification 2. Obtain both badges 3. Observe final success screen
Expected Output: Both verifications complete successfully. Final success screen: It's time to get verified with gold badge. All 6 benefits displayed. Premium perks: Double Likes (10)

Input: 1. Complete verification 2. Tap Continue button 3. Observe feed and profile updates
Expected Output: Smooth transition to main feed. Verification badge visible on own profile. Other verified/unverified users visible. Badges visible on other profiles. App functions normally post-verification

Input: Test on: iPhone SE (375x667), iPhone 12 (390x844), iPhone 14 Pro Max (430x932), Android devices. Check button accessibility, text readability, frame positioning, landscape orientation
Expected Output: All elements fit properly on all screen sizes. No overflow or improper scaling. Text readable at all sizes. Buttons accessible. Landscape mode works properly

Input: Review all user-facing text: instructions, error messages, success messages, button text, modal content. Assess clarity and understandability
Expected Output: Clear, concise, actionable instructions. Helpful error messages (not confusing). Motivating success messages. Clear button CTAs. Easy to understand modal content

Input: Verify primary buttons most prominent. Secondary buttons less prominent. Error messages noticeable. Success messages positive. Instructions clear
Expected Output: Primary buttons: larger, brighter color. Secondary buttons: smaller or outline style. Error messages: red highlight/icon. Success messages: green checkmark. Clear instruction placement

Input: Measure contrast ratios for: Primary text, Button text, Error text, Link text. Compare against WCAG AA standards (4.5:1 minimum)
Expected Output: All elements meet WCAG AA contrast standards (≥ 4.5:1). Light backgrounds with dark text readable. Buttons have sufficient contrast

Input: Test forward flow (Verify taps). Test backward flow (device back button). Test skip flow. Test retry flow
Expected Output: Each tap advances flow properly. Back button returns to previous screen. Skip buttons successfully skip. Retry buttons return to capture. Intuitive navigation

Input: Measure cold load on: Get Verified screen, Camera capture screen, Processing screen, Results screen
Expected Output: Get Verified: < 1 second. Camera capture: < 2 seconds. Processing: display immediately. Results: < 3 seconds

Input: Measure time for: Camera permission request, Initialize camera feed, Capture image
Expected Output: Permission dialog: < 500ms. Live preview: < 2 seconds. Capture: < 500ms. All processes responsive

Input: Measure time for: Local face detection, Image quality check, Document type detection, Server verification
Expected Output: Local face detection: < 1 second. Quality check: < 1 second. Document detection: < 2 seconds. Server processing: loading state shown

Input: Monitor memory during: Start verification, Initialize camera, Process multiple images, Complete verification
Expected Output: Baseline + 50MB acceptable. Camera: + 30MB. Multiple attempts: no leaks. Post-verification: return to baseline

Input: 1. Capture selfie 2. Capture document 3. Monitor transmission 4. Verify storage 5. Test deletion
Expected Output: Images transmitted via HTTPS/TLS encryption. Local storage encrypted. Server storage encrypted and access-controlled. User deletion request permanently removes images

Input: 1. Generate face template from selfie 2. Perform verification 3. Monitor data retention 4. Verify third-party services
Expected Output: Face templates encrypted in storage. Comparison secure (client-side if possible). Clear retention policy. Third-party services verified secure

Input: 1. Deny camera permission 2. Revoke permission after grant 3. Grant permission 4. Background/foreground app
Expected Output: Permission denial: graceful error, no crash. Permission revocation: graceful handling. Permission grant: camera initializes. Background: camera stops, resumes on foreground

Input: 1. Run long verification process 2. Attempt network sniffing 3. Verify user auth before submission 4. Test CSRF protection
Expected Output: Session remains valid during long processes. Session encrypted against sniffing. User authentication verified before docs submitted. CSRF tokens used in forms

Input: 1. Submit wrong file format 2. Submit corrupted image 3. Submit unexpected document 4. Submit oversized file
Expected Output: Wrong format: rejected with error. Corrupted file: rejected. Unexpected document: rejected with guidance. Oversized: compressed or rejected

Input: 1. Tap verify 2. Deny camera permission in dialog 3. Observe error 4. Tap Enable Settings 5. Enable in Settings 6. Return to app
Expected Output: Graceful error message displayed. Explanation provided with option to enable in settings. Settings app opens to camera permissions. Can retry after enabling

Input: 1. Start verification 2. Disconnect network 3. Attempt to submit image 4. Reconnect network 5. Tap retry
Expected Output: Error message displayed. Retry available. Requests queued when disconnected. Sent when connection restored. No data loss

Input: 1. Trigger 500 error 2. Trigger 502 error 3. Trigger 503 error 4. Trigger generic error
Expected Output: 500: Technical issue message. 502: Temporarily unavailable message. 503: Try again later message. Generic: Error with retry option. All handled gracefully

Input: 1. Test unavailable camera 2. Force camera crash 3. Force camera close unexpectedly
Expected Output: Unavailable: error message with gallery alternative. Crash: app doesn't crash, error shown. Unexpected close: graceful recovery. User guided to alternatives

Input: 1. Free device with <100MB space 2. Attempt capture 3. Attempt gallery import
Expected Output: Warning displayed before capture. Error shown if space insufficient. User suggestion to free space. Gallery import shows error if insufficient space

Input: 1. Single face in frame 2. Multiple faces in frame 3. No face in frame 4. Partial face 5. Animal/object
Expected Output: Single face: 100% detected. Multiple faces: detected with retry prompt. No face: error message. Partial: error message. Animal/object: no detection, error shown

Input: 1. Document fully in frame 2. Document partially in frame 3. Rotated document 4. Document too close 5. Document too far
Expected Output: Fully in frame: accepted. Partially: rejected with guidance. Rotated: accepted if readable. Too close: rejected with distance prompt. Too far: rejected with closer prompt

Input: 1. US Passport 2. US Driver's License 3. ID Card 4. Invalid document
Expected Output: Passport: >95% identified correctly. License: >95% identified. ID Card: >95% identified. Invalid: >99% rejected correctly

Input: 1. Not expired document 2. Expired document 3. Expiration not readable 4. Expiring soon

Input: 1. Enable screen reader 2. Navigate through verification flow 3. Test all UI elements

Input: 1. Enable VoiceOver 2. Navigate buttons 3. Capture image 4. Observe error/success messages
